rows.evidence: Implementation matches checklist:855 verbatim ('central_subjects: lexicon/Ghost B entities and profile concepts'); 673 cards built 2026-07-13 but pair cards miss the latent family entirely (PLAN_CRITIQUE:103) — an input-coverage gap, not a schema gap.

one_word_two_meanings: 'latent' — parent_chunks.latent_concepts (retrieval capture) vs DiscoverResponse.latent_topics + trace latent_terms (graph-discovery emerging signals; models/schemas.py:997,878) — unrelated, never merge
'concept contract' — P2.1 universal/lexicon contract (corpus_lexicon.py) vs P2.2/T-HOOK-2 Ghost A summary capture contract (summary_semantics.py)

'concepts' bare — summary_tree.concepts (node) vs documents.doc_profile.concepts (doc) vs Qdrant doc_summaries payload 'concepts' vs parent concept_tags; and central_subjects renamed to 'subjects' in slim payloads
duplicate_implementation_risk: doc-level concept derivation: doc_profile.concepts already IS the L4 tree node's derive_node_concepts output — S5 must extend, not re-derive
latent-into-cards: candidate_latent_subjects already implements the latent->card path; merging into central_subjects or adding a second latent seed would duplicate and break the corroborate-only rule

alias registries: corpus_lexicon aliases (identity evidence) + latent_concepts[].aliases (user phrasings, currently read by nothing) — S5's versioned alias registry must fold BOTH, not become a third store
semantic_profile: planned as an aggregation over existing parent_chunks.semantic_chunk_type — build as projection in corpus_lexicon, not a new extraction lane
